patch.value: The four workspace-customization commands (init, config, schema, profile) are the only remaining top-level family with no consolidation child under pm-n7rr and no explicit entry in the ADR pm-pbyu mapping. They overlap: schema manages types/statuses/fields/presets, profile applies archetype bundles that include schema+config+templates, config manages settings keys, init bootstraps all of it. Decide whether they stay four nouns (each arguably a domain) or consolidate (e.g. schema+profile under one customization noun) toward the ~15-noun target. This is a mapping/decision task: outcome is the grammar-table entry plus any follow-up wiring items, not a rename itself.
Map the workspace-customization family (init/config/schema/profile) in the target noun grammar: keep as distinct nouns or merge under one customization noun

ADR pm-pbyu's grammar table gains an explicit mapping for init, config, schema, and profile (kept, merged, or verb-moved) with rationale; overlaps are resolved on paper (schema presets vs profile archetypes vs config keys); if any spelling moves, follow-up wiring items are filed under pm-n7rr with alias shims per pm-0z7n
